2604.18572.title: Back into Plato's Cave: Examining Cross-modal Representational Convergence at Scale
2604.18572.paper_summary: The Platonic Representation Hypothesis suggests that neural networks trained on different modalities (e.g., text and images) align and eventually converge toward the same representation of reality. If true, this has significant implications for whether modality choice matters at all. We show that the experimental evidence for this hypothesis is fragile and depends critically on the evaluation regime. Alignment is measured using mutual nearest neighbors on small datasets ($\approx$1K samples) and degrades substantially as the dataset is scaled to millions of samples. The alignment that remains between model representations reflects coarse semantic overlap rather than consistent fine-grained structure. Moreover, the evaluations in Huh et al. are done in a one-to-one image-caption setting, a constraint that breaks down in realistic many-to-many settings and further reduces alignment. We also find that the reported trend of stronger language models increasingly aligning with vision does not appear to hold for newer models. Overall, our findings suggest that the current evidence for cross-modal representational convergence is considerably weaker than subsequent works have taken it to be. Models trained on different modalities may learn equally rich representations of the world, just not the same one.
2604.18572.paper_summary_zh: 柏拉圖表徵假說認為，訓練於不同模態（例如，文本和圖像）的神經網絡會對齊並最終收斂到相同的現實表徵。<lb/>如果這是真的，那麼這對於模態選擇是否重要有著重大的影響。<lb/>我們顯示這一假說的實驗證據是脆弱的，並且在很大程度上依賴於評估制度。<lb/>對齊是通過在小型數據集上使用互相最近鄰來測量的（約1K樣本），並且在數據集擴展到數百萬樣本時顯著下降。<lb/>模型表徵之間剩餘的對齊反映的是粗略的語義重疊，而不是一致的細粒度結構。<lb/>此外，Huh等人的評估是在一對一的圖像-標題設置中進行的，這一限制在現實的多對多設置中崩潰，進一步減少了對齊。<lb/>我們還發現，報告的強語言模型與視覺越來越對齊的趨勢似乎不適用於更新的模型。<lb/>總體而言，我們的研究結果表明，目前對跨模態表徵收斂的證據遠比後續工作所認為的要弱得多。<lb/>在不同模態上訓練的模型可能會學習到同樣豐富的世界表徵，只是並不是相同的那一個。
2604.18572.author: A. Sophia Koepke et.al.
2604.18572.authors: A. Sophia Koepke, Daniil Zverev, Shiry Ginosar, Alexei A. Efros
